items.summary: Course cover: Hallo Deutsch Plus Neu A1 training booklet with audio CD.
Inside cover repeating the Hallo Deutsch Plus Neu A1 title with publisher logos.
Lektion 1 contents (Erste Kontakte): greetings, family, cities, counting, grammar, games.
Lektion 2 contents (So wohne ich): rooms, drinks, pets, origins, grammar, games.
Lektion 3 contents (Mein Alltag) plus appendix with transcripts, sources, country map.
Series components overview with prices and a legend of symbols used in the book.
Greetings practice: choose the correctly spelled greeting and unscramble jumbled greeting words.
Greetings by time of day, greeting/farewell for people, and matching questions to answers.
Complete a greeting dialogue and write missing questions about name, age, and hometown.
Fill your own profile, then order a DJ interview and complete his profile.
Write a penpal email, then give German articles for family-member nouns.
Sort relatives into family categories, present the Beckmann family, and read a family tree.
Family-tree questions identifying relatives and matching people to their number of siblings.
State your own siblings, then read about Familie Zidek and answer comprehension questions.
Introduce four young people from cards, then take notes on a director interview.
Write a school-magazine article and build polite Sie-form questions about the director.
Complete a profile from listening, then match cities to their German-speaking regions.
Match more cities to regions and answer yes/no questions about where cities lie.
Listen to identify six German-speaking cities, then match car number plates to cities.
Write short dialogues about where five young people live and whether they like it.
Write numbers as words and digits, then listen and mark lottery and spoken numbers.
Complete -zehn/-zig number endings, then listen and choose the correct prices.
Listen and note four people's mobile numbers, then find hidden number words.
Present-tense conjugation table and gap-fills for the verbs sein, heißen and wohnen.
Fill in personal pronouns, match names to er/sie, and form questions.
Form questions, unscramble word-order sentences, answer, and fill prepositions in/bei/von.
Answer location questions and fill definite and possessive articles for family nouns.
Listening-response and pronunciation drills on names, age, siblings and where people live.
Memory game: cut-out cards with family have-sentences to match into pairs.
Cut-out sheet of sixteen identical card backs labeled Wortschatz Familie for the game.
Sentence-building game: cut-out question-word, verb and subject cards to form questions.
Cut-out sheet of card backs labeled Grammatik Wo? Wie? Wer? for the game.
Sentence-building game: colored subject, verb and complement cards to form statements.
Cut-out sheet of card backs labeled Grammatik Aussagesätze for the sentence game.
Syllable puzzle: cut-out syllable cards to reassemble into family and describing words.
Cut-out sheet of twenty-four identical Silbenrätsel card backs for the syllable game.
Chapter opener 'So wohne ich': match ten furniture words to numbered picture items.
Answer with nein/kein from pictures, then match household objects to the pronouns er, sie, es.
Tick boxes about your home's type, location, size and features, then write a short description.
Match rooms to adjectives by listening, build room dialogues, and rate rooms with adjectives.
Read Patrick's room description, mark five true/false statements, then describe your own room.
Fill gaps in Tina's room text, then listen and complete a real-estate agency form.
Offer drinks to six people and write short 'would you rather drink' dialogues from pictures.
Complete a greeting-and-drink dialogue, pick the odd drink out, and match drink questions to answers.
Pets: write what two girls say about a kitten, then build sentences about owning animals.
Pets: work out who owns which animal, then read about Melanie's dog and fill gaps.
Give exaggerated 'I have many pets' answers, then match countries with their language and capital.
State where seven classmates come from and say, in Q&A form, what language each speaks.
Build sentences on where seven people come from and match each person to their nationality.
Complete a dialogue asking Efer about his origin, then fill an interview table by listening.
Reconstruct the interviewer's questions to Herr Dupont, then complete a quiz-show profile by listening.
Grammar drills: conjugate the present tense of sprechen, haben, kommen and trinken.
Grammar drills: conjugate trinken and mögen and practise the möchte(n) form in sentences.
Grammar drills: choose Wer or Was, form questions, and supply articles and dir/Ihnen forms.
Grammar fill-in: dative pronouns (mir/dir/Ihnen) and accusative articles einen/eine/ein.
Grammar fill-in: Wo/Woher questions, in/aus prepositions, negation with nicht/kein.
Grammar fill-in: negation (kein/nicht) and inversion word-order transformations.
Listen-and-respond speaking drills plus intonation repetition (furniture, drinks, pets, countries).
Memory game: match room pictures with adjectives describing the home.
Reverse side of the p.59 memory cards, all labelled 'Wortschatz: Wohnung'.
Cut-out word cards (subjects, verbs, articles, nouns) to build accusative sentences.
Reverse side of the p.61 game cards, all labelled 'Grammatik: Akkusativ'.
Cut-out cards (question words, verbs, subjects) to build Wo/Woher/Was questions.
Reverse side of the p.63 game cards, all labelled 'Wo? Woher? Was?'.
Cut-out syllable cards to combine into Lektion 2 vocabulary words.
Reverse side of the p.65 syllable cards, all labelled 'Silbenrätsel'.
Lektion 3 opener: match breakfast-food pictures with words; identify what is missing.
Sort breakfast nouns by article, build meal sentences, describe break-time snacks.
Match Q&A, answer offers with negation, complete a snack-offer dialogue.
Reading on a healthy breakfast with true/false, then label school-supply pictures.
Negation answers about school items plus listening tasks for sentences and prices.
Plural forms of school objects, then fill accusative articles in Hast-du Q&A.
Pack your Ranzen, say what school items you need, and hear what teachers teach.
Say how you find each school subject, then listen for which subjects Patrick likes.
React to TV shows: sort them by preference and describe each with adjectives.
Write what's on TV from a schedule, then listen and complete the programme table.
Answer questions about your TV habits, then read three teens' views on television.
Tell the time: match clocks to phrases, listen for exact times, build separable verbs.
Write sentences about your morning and describe Marie Claire's full daily schedule.
Answer questions about your day, then read a text about Marsi the Martian.
Comprehension questions on the Marsi text, then complete a daily-routine dialogue.
Grammar: conjugate irregular present-tense verbs essen, nehmen, geben, sehen, fahren.
Grammar: separable-verb conjugation, build sentences, identify subject vs. object (accusative).
Grammar: nominative vs. accusative and the accusative articles einen/ein/eine, den/die/das.
Grammar: accusative articles, accusative pronouns ihn/sie/es, and directional in + accusative.
Grammar: answer with negation using kein and nicht (food, school items, opinions).
Grammar: temporal prepositions um/am and forming time questions (Wann, Wie lange).
Listen-and-respond speaking drills (food, school, TV, times) plus intonation repetition practice.
Memory game: match food and drink picture-cards with their sentence-completion word cards.
Reverse side of the p.89 memory cards, all labelled 'Wortschatz: Essen und Trinken'.
Cut-out word cards to build sentences giving opinions about TV programmes.
Reverse side of the p.91 game cards, all labelled 'Grammatik: Akkusativ'.
Cut-out word cards to build daily-routine sentences with separable verbs.
Reverse side of the p.93 game cards, all labelled 'Trennbare Verben'.
Cut-out syllable cards to combine into Lektion 3 vocabulary words.
Reverse side of the p.95 syllable cards, all labelled 'Silbenrätsel'.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte), Lektion 1: introduction and interview dialogues.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte), Lektion 1: numbers, prices, phone numbers, Q&A drills.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte), Lektion 2: house-hunting and country/language dialogues.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte), Lektion 2: furniture, drinks, pets and language drills.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte), Lektion 3: school subjects, TV and telling time.
Audio transcripts (Hörtexte) Lektion 3 plus CD credits and copyright.
Picture credits (Bildquellenverzeichnis) listing image sources by page number.
Reference map of German-speaking countries: regions, waters, rivers, mountains, cities.
